terms.definition: The arc of movement available at a joint, measured actively or passively in degrees.
terms.exampleSentence: Right shoulder range of motion was reduced in flexion to ninety degrees.
terms.contextNotes: Documented as AROM and PROM with goniometric values.

terms.definition: Standardised assessment of muscle strength against gravity and applied resistance.
terms.exampleSentence: Manual muscle testing revealed grade four out of five hip abductor strength.
terms.contextNotes: Graded using Oxford scale from zero to five.

terms.definition: Six-point ordinal scale grading manual muscle strength from zero to five.
terms.exampleSentence: Quadriceps strength was documented as four out of five on the Oxford scale.
terms.contextNotes: Grade 3 = full ROM against gravity without resistance.

terms.definition: Systematic observation of walking pattern, identifying deviations across the gait cycle.
terms.exampleSentence: Gait analysis showed a reduced stance phase on the affected limb.
terms.contextNotes: Includes spatial, temporal and kinematic parameters.

terms.definition: Special test assessing anterior cruciate ligament integrity at twenty to thirty degrees of knee flexion.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive Lachman's test with soft end feel suggested ACL rupture.
terms.contextNotes: Most sensitive clinical test for ACL injury.

terms.definition: Special test for meniscal pathology using knee flexion with rotation and extension.
terms.exampleSentence: A painful click on McMurray test indicated medial meniscal injury.
terms.contextNotes: Internal rotation tests lateral meniscus; external tests medial.

terms.definition: Neural tension test raising the extended leg to assess sciatic nerve irritation.
terms.exampleSentence: Straight leg raise reproduced posterior thigh pain at forty degrees.
terms.contextNotes: Reproduction of pain between 30-70¬į is suggestive of nerve root irritation.

terms.definition: Special test for hip flexor tightness performed in supine with the contralateral hip flexed.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive Thomas test indicated bilateral hip flexor tightness.
terms.contextNotes: Modified version isolates rectus femoris versus iliopsoas tightness.

terms.definition: Pelvic drop on the unsupported side during single-leg stance, indicating hip abductor weakness.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive Trendelenburg sign was noted on the right.
terms.contextNotes: Reflects ipsilateral hip abductor weakness or pain inhibition.

terms.definition: Radiating pain along the sciatic nerve distribution, typically from lumbar nerve root compression.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient reported left-sided sciatica radiating to the calf.
terms.contextNotes: Most often caused by L4/5 or L5/S1 disc prolapse.

terms.definition: Painful progressive restriction of glenohumeral movement due to capsular contracture.
terms.exampleSentence: Global capsular restriction was consistent with frozen shoulder.
terms.contextNotes: External rotation is typically most restricted.

terms.definition: Inflammatory and fibrotic thickening of the glenohumeral joint capsule causing global stiffness.
terms.exampleSentence: MRI features were consistent with adhesive capsulitis.
terms.contextNotes: Three classical phases: freezing, frozen, thawing.

terms.definition: Complete discontinuity of the anterior cruciate ligament, often during a non-contact pivoting injury.
terms.exampleSentence: Mechanism of injury and instability were consistent with ACL rupture.
terms.contextNotes: Often associated with bone bruising and meniscal injury.

terms.definition: Degenerative or inflammatory condition of the plantar fascia causing medial heel pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Morning first-step pain was characteristic of plantar fasciitis.
terms.contextNotes: Often associated with calf tightness and obesity.

terms.definition: Degenerative or overuse condition of a tendon characterised by pain and impaired function.
terms.exampleSentence: Ultrasound confirmed mid-portion Achilles tendinopathy.
terms.contextNotes: Managed primarily with progressive loading rehabilitation.

terms.definition: Stretch or tear of a ligament caused by abnormal joint movement.
terms.exampleSentence: The mechanism produced a grade two lateral ankle sprain.
terms.contextNotes: Graded I to III according to severity of ligament disruption.

terms.definition: Stretch or tear of a muscle or tendon, often from overload or sudden contraction.
terms.exampleSentence: Acute deceleration produced a grade one hamstring strain.
terms.contextNotes: Distinguish from sprain: strain is muscle/tendon, sprain is ligament.

terms.definition: Permanent shortening of muscle, tendon or other soft tissue limiting joint range of motion.
terms.exampleSentence: A fixed flexion contracture of fifteen degrees was noted at the knee.
terms.contextNotes: Common after prolonged immobilisation or in upper motor neurone lesions.

terms.definition: Excessive posterior convex curvature of the thoracic spine in the sagittal plane.
terms.exampleSentence: Postural assessment revealed an exaggerated thoracic kyphosis.
terms.contextNotes: Cobb angle greater than 40¬į is considered hyperkyphotic.

terms.definition: Lateral curvature of the spine with vertebral rotation, measured by Cobb angle.
terms.exampleSentence: Adam's forward bend test revealed a thoracic rib hump consistent with scoliosis.
terms.contextNotes: Cobb angle ‚Č•10¬į required for diagnosis.

terms.definition: Anterior convex curvature of the spine, normally found in cervical and lumbar regions.
terms.exampleSentence: Standing posture showed an increased lumbar lordosis.
terms.contextNotes: Excessive lordosis is associated with anterior pelvic tilt.

terms.definition: Chronic widespread musculoskeletal pain syndrome with fatigue and central sensitisation.
terms.exampleSentence: She met diagnostic criteria for fibromyalgia following two years of widespread pain.
terms.contextNotes: Managed with graded exercise, education and pacing.

terms.definition: Cervical soft-tissue injury from rapid acceleration-deceleration forces, commonly in road traffic collisions.
terms.exampleSentence: Neck pain emerged the morning after a rear-end whiplash injury.
terms.contextNotes: Classified using the Quebec Task Force WAD grades 0-IV.

terms.definition: Cervical nerve root compression producing dermatomal pain, paraesthesia or weakness in the upper limb.
terms.exampleSentence: Spurling's test reproduced symptoms consistent with C6 cervical radiculopathy.
terms.contextNotes: C6 and C7 are the most commonly affected levels.

terms.definition: Anterior or posterior translation of one vertebral body relative to an adjacent one.
terms.exampleSentence: Lateral radiograph showed a grade one L5 on S1 spondylolisthesis.
terms.contextNotes: Graded by Meyerding classification (I-V).

terms.definition: Diffuse anterior knee pain aggravated by loaded knee flexion such as stairs or squatting.
terms.exampleSentence: Pain on stair descent was characteristic of patellofemoral pain syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Often associated with hip abductor weakness and dynamic knee valgus.

terms.definition: Mechanical compression of subacromial structures during shoulder elevation, producing pain.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive Hawkins-Kennedy test indicated subacromial impingement.
terms.contextNotes: Painful arc typically between 60¬į and 120¬į of abduction.

terms.definition: Tendinopathy at the common extensor origin on the lateral humeral epicondyle.
terms.exampleSentence: Resisted wrist extension reproduced pain consistent with lateral epicondylitis.
terms.contextNotes: Most common chronic elbow condition in adults aged 35-55.

terms.definition: Median nerve compression at the wrist causing thumb-side hand paraesthesia and weakness.
terms.exampleSentence: Nocturnal paraesthesia in the radial three digits suggested carpal tunnel syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Phalen's and Tinel's tests support diagnosis; nerve conduction confirms.

terms.definition: Written handover document summarising assessment, intervention and onward plan at end of episode.
terms.exampleSentence: The discharge summary was sent to the GP within forty-eight hours.
terms.contextNotes: Core OET Writing task format for physiotherapy candidates.
